implementation.definition: Software development or assembling according to previously created design.
implementation.modularized_design: Interaction among system components (objects, packages, components) must be implemented using certain standards such as SOAP/XML, CORBA, or COM.

testing.definition: A process of identifying system defects.

components.test_cases: Specifies starting and ending state, and events to which the software must respond.
components.test_data: Data in the defined starting state, which must reach a defined ending state after being exposed to a certain event (input).

kinds_of_system_tests.description: Tests individual methods, classes, or components before they are integrated with other software.
kinds_of_system_tests.defect_example: A method for calculating taxes outputs incorrect results.

kinds_of_system_tests.description: Evaluates the behavior of a group of methods or classes (interface compatibility, unexpected parameter values or state interaction, run-time exceptions). Applied to system or sub-system.
kinds_of_system_tests.defect_example: Results of retrieving a record significantly differ when real database as opposed to dummy database is retrieved.

kinds_of_system_tests.description: Evaluates if software works but fails some usability requirements (easy to learn, use & navigate, task efficiency).
Involves users to determine if the system overall fulfills user requirements.

programmers: Testing buddies can test other programmers’ code
users: Volunteers for testing beta version of software

versioning.description: Test version that is incomplete but ready for some level of testing; dummy data used.
Test version that is stable enough to be tested by end users for an extended period of time; real data used.
System version that is formally released to users or made operational for long-term use.

system_deployment.definition: Activities to make a new system operational.

data_porting_and_conversion.note: Listed in the outline and as a deployment activity, but the corresponding slide contains only the title with no additional details provided in the document.
